free_text: Fast neutron radiative capture cross sections
and d-wave strength functions
Pulsed Van-de-Graaff accelerator EG-1
Metal lithium targets were used
Resolution 2 nsec/m
Liquid scintillation detector with
B-10 loaded toluene, volume 17 litres for
prompt gamma-rays detection.
B-10 plug and Na-I(Tl)
Li-6 thin glass scintillator
Amplitude weighting method and saturated
resonance method were used
For determination of the absolute value of the
capture cross sections the standard (Gold) or the
saturated resonance method was used.
Corrections have been made for isotopic impurities,
multiple neutron scattering and resonance
self-shielding, for background.
Total uncertainty
Correction in SUBENT 40621033
Page 518 was added in REFERENCE.
Upper -> lower case correction.
ERR-T -> DATA-ERR -partial uncertainties are not given.
Reaction was corrected in Subents 007 - 034.
Oxide Dy(2)O(3) powder 65.5 % enrichment,
diameter 40 mm, thickness 0.00597  nuclei/b
From the author Yurlov, 1982-03-15
Data are on Fig.1 of C,81GRENOB,,518,1981
Oxide DY(2)O(3) powder, 92.2 % enrichment,
diameter 40 mm, thickness 0.00903 nuclei/b
From the author Yurlov, 1982-03-15
Data are on Fig.1 of C,81GRENOB,,518,1981
Oxide, powder, 95.1 % enrichment,
thickness 0.00897 nuclei/b, diameter 40 mm

From the author Yurlov, 1982-03-15
Data are on Fig.1 of C,81GRENOB,,518,1981
Maximum likelihood method in the framework of the
statistical theory of nuclear reactions was used.
d-wave neutron strength functions were determined
from the experimental average radiative neutron
capture cross-section curves in the framework
of the statistical theory
